subject: Social Science (Civics)
overall_learning_objectives: Learn why secularism is important in a diverse country
Understand the relationship between religion and the state
Know how the Indian Constitution supports secularism
Recognise the importance of equality among religions

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Secularism
student_activities: Students list different religions they know
Discuss how people celebrate different festivals

lesson_plan.title: Importance of Secularism
student_activities: Discuss why respecting different religions is important

lesson_plan.title: State and Religion
student_activities: Give examples where the government treats religions equally
Discuss why the government should not favor any religion

lesson_plan.title: Indian Secularism
student_activities: Write one example showing religious freedom in India
Discuss how people of different religions live together

lesson_plan.title: Role of the Constitution
student_activities: List two rights related to religious freedom
Explain why equality before the law is important

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Chapter Summary
student_activities: Write two points about why secularism is important

teacher_tip: Encourage students to respect different religions and explain how secularism helps maintain unity in a diverse country like India.
